message:
[medium] notion: reject api_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_api_url_for_auth rejected non-api.notion.com hosts in
direct-token mode but still accepted api_url values carrying query
strings, fragments, or userinfo. Because the validator returns
parsed.to_string() (which preserves all of those components), a
configure payload like `https://api.notion.com/?leak=attacker.com`
survived validation and then got concatenated into every request
URL via `format!("{api_url}/v1/...", ...)`, corrupting the final
path and leaking the attacker-controlled query into upstream calls.
`?leak=x` is a particularly bad case because reqwest preserves the
query and the `/v1/...` suffix becomes part of the value.

- validate_api_url_for_auth now rejects query, fragment, and
  userinfo components at config time, matching the stricter
  hygiene already applied in whatsapp::normalize_base_url and
  stripe::api_url_policy.
- Three new unit tests cover the query, fragment, and userinfo
  rejection paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
